title: Rich Molten Venezuelan Sausages Skewers
description: A rich and flavorful Venezuelan dish featuring sausages, glaze, and a show-off presentation, perfect for special occasions, this recipe is also shojin-friendly as it does not contain any animal by-products in the glaze or seasonings, making it suitable for a variety of dietary preferences.

ingredients.name: Venezuelan sausages
ingredients.notes: preferably with a high meat content

instructions: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat, making sure it reaches a safe internal temperature of 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together olive oil, garlic, brown sugar, soy sauce, apple cider vinegar, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper to make the glaze.
Thread a sausage onto each wooden skewer, leaving a small space at the top.
Brush the glaze all over the sausages, making sure they are fully coated.
Place the skewers on the grill and cook for 5 minutes per side, or until the sausages are cooked through and slightly charred.
Remove the skewers from the grill and let them rest for a few minutes.
Serve the skewers hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
To ensure food safety, use a food thermometer to check the internal temperature of the sausages, which should reach 160 degrees Fahrenheit.
Let the skewers cool for a few minutes before serving to allow the juices to redistribute.
For an added touch, serve with a side of grilled or roasted vegetables.
